channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Indie Boards & Cards' The Magnus Archives Mysteries Kickstarter is Fully Funded in 10 Minutes
item.content:encoded:
Indie Boards & Cards' newest crowdfunding campaign is a runaway success. The studio's new project, The Magnus Archives Mysteries, adapting Rusty Quill's award-winning horror anthology podcast, reached its initial funding goal in just 10 minutes.

According to the campaign page, The Magnus Archives Mysteries is a collection of six interactive mysteries set in the world of The Magnus Archives. Players take on the roles of Interim Archival Assistants unraveling these mysteries. Said mysteries include a new statement and research materials dug up by other members of the Magnus Institute.

Generally, The Magnus Archives Mysteries is similar to Indie Boards & Cards' other series, Sherlock Files. Each case is standalone and can be completed solo or with a small group. The materials in the case all contain critical clues and puzzles, similar to an escape room with no time limit. Once you've reached your conclusion, you can submit your answer to a website and see if you're right.

The six cases were designed by Sydney Engelstein, whose extensive tabletop experience includes Aeon's End, Space Cadets, and The Magnus Protocol Mysteries, an adaptation of the sequel series. For dedicated Magnus fans, each of the case's statements include new audio recordings by podcast co-creator Jonny Sims, reprising his role as The Head Archivist.

The pledge tiers for The Magnus Archives Mysteries are as follows:

  • A Single Case for $20: One case board game, The Hanged Man.
  • Base Pledge for $50: Three cases: The Resurrection Men, Curtain Call, and Into The Hive
  • Expansion Pledge for $90: Everything in the Base Pledge tier and three more cases: The Hanged Man, Contagion, and The Labyrinth
  • All the Cases for $180: Everything in the Expansion Pledge tier and all six cases from The Magnus Protocol Mysteries
  • All the Cases, Plus More for $195: Everything in the All the Cases tier, plus an enamel pin and Sticker Pack

At the time of writing, The Magnus Archives Mysteries has raised $97,375, almost ten times its initial funding goal of $10,000. The campaign is set to end on April 10, with products expected to ship on January 2027.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/indie-boards-cards-magnus-archives-mysteries-kickstarter-is-fully-funded-in-10
